title: PHONON · Glossary
subtitle: Terms and symbols across the phonon DOS reproduction — physics, the E(3)NN, and the FORGE Bayesian + information-theory layers.

terms.def: A quantized collective vibration of the crystal lattice — the lattice's normal modes treated as quasiparticles. A cell of $N$ atoms has $3N$ vibrational modes per wavevector.

The distribution of vibrational modes over frequency: how many modes lie in each interval $[\omega,\omega+d\omega]$. The 51-bin curve the model predicts. After normalization it is a probability distribution over frequency.
The DOS-weighted average frequency — the headline scalar the reproduction is scored on. Reaching 70% of test materials within 10% relative error on $\bar\omega$ is the paper's benchmark.
Density Functional Perturbation Theory — the first-principles method that computed the ground-truth phonon DOS for all 1,524 training crystals. Expensive; the model is the fast surrogate.
The vibrational heat capacity, obtained by integrating the DOS against a per-mode kernel. A pure functional of $g(\omega)$ — derivable from any predicted DOS once renormalized to $3N$.
The lattice-vibration contribution to entropy — another $\int g(\omega)\cdot\text{kernel}\,d\omega$ functional of the DOS, so it comes for free from a predicted spectrum.

The Shannon entropy of the normalized DOS treated as a distribution. Low for sharp, peaked spectra (e.g. CsI); high for broad, many-mode ones. An information-theoretic feature unique to a distribution-valued target.

A single temperature scale summarizing the spectrum's stiffness, taken from moments of $g(\omega)$ (e.g. $\langle\omega^2\rangle$ or $\langle\ln\omega\rangle$). 'Effective' because the DOS has no branch labels to isolate the true acoustic cutoff.
A frequency window with no modes, separating acoustic from optical branches. Recoverable from the predicted DOS even when peak amplitudes are mispredicted; relevant to phonon-phonon scattering.

A network whose features and operations are equivariant to 3D rotations, translations, and inversion. Crystal symmetry is built in by construction rather than learned, which is what makes ~1,200 examples enough.
Rotate the input crystal and the internal geometric features rotate identically, leaving the scalar DOS output invariant. Removes the need for ~500-fold rotational data augmentation.
The symmetry 'type' of a feature under $O(3)$ — scalars ($l=0$), vectors ($l=1$), and so on. Here features carry $l=0$ and $l=1$ irreps, capped at $l_{max}=1$, with multiplicity $\text{mul}=32$.
The angular basis functions of the convolution filters — they encode edge direction in a rotation-covariant way.
Not free weights: a learned radial function times spherical harmonics of the edge direction. Tensor-producing this with neighbor features yields rotation-correct messages.
Atoms bond to all neighbors (including periodic images) within $r_{max}=5\,\text{\AA}$, defining the edges of the periodic graph.

Each atom's input feature is a 118-length vector, zero except at its element, where it holds the atomic mass. Mass is a continuous physical handle that survives even for elements never seen in training.
A linear map taking the 118-length mass/type one-hots to 64 scalar features per atom, before the equivariant convolutions.
The equivariance-safe activation: scalars get an ordinary nonlinearity (SiLU); vector features are multiplied by a learned sigmoid gate, since a pointwise nonlinearity would break equivariance.
The specific Convolution + Gated Block layer design (Miller et al.) the architecture is built from, as implemented in the e3nn library.
The crystal cast as nodes (atoms) and edges (neighbors within $r_{max}$, including images in adjacent cells); each edge stores its displacement vector $\vec r_{ab}$.

The sole training objective — mean squared error between predicted $\hat g$ and DFPT $g$, over the 51 bins. No regularization terms, no uncertainty machinery: the plain point-estimate loss the FORGE layers improve on.
The optimizer (lr 0.005, weight decay 0.05). Weight decay is decoupled parameter shrinkage in the optimizer — not a term inside the loss.
The model output is divided by its per-row maximum, so training is on DOS shape in $[0,1]$. The absolute $\int g\,d\omega=3N$ scale is restored downstream for thermodynamics.

The reference recipe runs a fixed epoch budget and keeps the last checkpoint, not the best-validation one — so the released model sits slightly past its own validation optimum (train MSE $\approx$ 0.002 vs valid $\approx$ 0.026).
The source of ~1,500 crystalline solids with computed phonon DOS. Our working set is 1,524 materials, split 1220 / 152 / 152 (train / test / val).

Elements absent from training (54 of 118: the lanthanides, actinides, noble gases, and the heaviest p-block). The model still produces a DOS for materials containing them via the continuous mass channel — the generalization the paper tests on Nd- and U-bearing compounds, and where Bayesian uncertainty should spike.

For an alloy $A_pB_{1-p}$, a single site's feature is a composition-weighted mix of two elements' encodings. Because the encoding is linear, the model predicts the whole alloy series at no extra training cost.

Virtual Crystal Approximation — a classical alloy method. Eq. 1 contains its essence (mass average $m_{VCA}=p\,m_A+(1-p)\,m_B$ plus a potential average) in one encoding, but without VCA's failure modes.
The structure database supplying the 4,346 unseen crystals used for the downstream high-$C_V$ screening demo (no DOS labels).
The headline test metric. The reproduction target is 70% of test materials below 10%.
A diagnostic: error should be uncorrelated with unit-cell size (no bias toward small/large cells). Reproduced as $\text{corr}\approx 0$.
A symmetric distance between predicted and true DOS as distributions — a more honest fidelity score than MSE, which ignores that the bins live on a frequency axis.
Distance along the frequency axis: a peak shifted one bin costs little, a peak in the wrong place costs a lot. MSE cannot see this; EMD respects the metric structure of $\omega$.
The penultimate latent vector the final linear layer reads. FORGE's posterior lives in $\varphi$-space; input-feature attribution is the bridge from $\varphi$ back to named inputs.

Last-Layer Laplace Approximation — a Gaussian posterior over the final layer's weights of the frozen model, giving calibrated uncertainty without retraining. The mk2 contribution; turns the bare point estimate into a credible band.
Uncertainty from limited/underdetermined data (reducible with more) — distinct from aleatoric noise. Expected to be largest on unseen elements and rare-element materials.
A Bayesian uncertainty band on the predicted DOS (and on derived $C_V$, $S_{vib}$). The $\texttt{band}$ slot in the inference output is reserved for it.
A reliability score combining accuracy, sweep spread, and epistemic uncertainty into one ranking signal, transferred from the genre and ATLAS work and adapted to regression.
The LLLA prior strength. Dragging $\tau$ reshapes the posterior live in the FORGE panel — high $\tau$ tightens toward the point estimate, low $\tau$ widens the band.

Per-input sensitivity of the epistemic uncertainty, via the chain rule $\tfrac{d\sigma}{d\varphi}\tfrac{d\varphi}{dx_k}$. On phonon, $x_k$ is an atom/element — so it names the physical origin of the uncertainty.

Calibration metrics for the predictive band: interval coverage (does the 90% band contain truth ~90% of the time) and a proper score over the full predictive distribution — the regression analogues of ECE.
